description: Top 25 Relationship Trigger Prompt Library. Pre-verified responses for the most common relationship scenarios — covering approximately 80% of relationship-based queries. Each entry includes trigger phrase, relationship context, likely emotion, methodology fix, and rational redirection.
source: NotebookLM cross-reference against My4Blocks methodology backbone and Relationship Interpretation Library

triggers.trigger_phrase: My partner ignored my message all day.
triggers.methodology_fix: Anger Formula (A = ET + S). The user is demanding their partner respond on their personal timeline — a should belief about another fallible person's behavior.
triggers.rational_redirection: My partner is a Fallible Human Being who is currently not responding. I would prefer a quick reply, but there is no law that they must follow my timeline.

triggers.trigger_phrase: I'm worried my partner is losing interest in me.
triggers.methodology_fix: Anxiety Formula (AX = WI + AW + ICSI). The user is projecting a future loss of connection that has not yet occurred.
triggers.rational_redirection: The future of this relationship is currently a story in my head. I will focus on Staying Now and breathe through the What If of abandonment.

triggers.trigger_phrase: My boss criticized my performance in front of the team.
triggers.methodology_fix: BS Equation (B = S). The user is treating the criticism as a global verdict on their worth as a person rather than feedback about a specific behavior.
triggers.rational_redirection: My supervisor's criticism is an Activating Event. I rate my behavior as needing improvement — Behavior equals Behavior — but I refuse to rate my entire Self as a failure.

triggers.trigger_phrase: My friends went out without inviting me.
triggers.methodology_fix: Depression Formula (D = H1 + H2 + N) — Pseudo-Need. The user is treating social inclusion as a necessity for their worth rather than a preference.
triggers.rational_redirection: I want their inclusion, but I do not need it to be a person of worth. I accept this social rejection without the story that I am worthless.

triggers.trigger_phrase: I feel terrible for the mean things I said during our fight.
triggers.methodology_fix: Guilt Formula (G = W1 + W2). The user is rating hurtful words as Wrongness and attaching Worthlessness to themselves as a person.
triggers.rational_redirection: I used hurtful words — Behavior equals Behavior — which was unhelpful. I am a Fallible Human Being. I will apologize and learn, but I am not a bad person.

triggers.trigger_phrase: My spouse just doesn't understand me at all.
triggers.methodology_fix: Anger Formula — Egocentric Thinking and Shoulds. The user is demanding their spouse see their perspective — an impossible standard placed on another fallible person.
triggers.rational_redirection: I would prefer my spouse understood my perspective, but demanding they must see it my way only disturbs me. I accept them as the Fallible Human Being they are.

triggers.trigger_phrase: My coworker is undermining my authority on this project.
triggers.methodology_fix: Anger Formula (A = ET + S). The coworker is acting from their own disturbance or ignorance — not from a judgment of the user's worth.
triggers.rational_redirection: My coworker is acting out of ignorance or disturbance. I would prefer they were supportive, but I will focus on the practical task rather than my rage.

triggers.trigger_phrase: What if I never find anyone else after this breakup?
triggers.methodology_fix: What If thinking and Hopelessness (H1). The user is making an Absolutistic Statement about a future that has not yet occurred.
triggers.rational_redirection: I cannot predict the future. I am mourning a loss right now, but the belief that I will always be alone is an Absolutistic Statement I choose to let go of.

triggers.trigger_phrase: My parents are constantly judging how I raise my kids.
triggers.methodology_fix: First Insight — 100% Responsibility. The user creates 100% of their frustration by demanding their parents stop judging. The judgment is the Activating Event, not the cause.
triggers.rational_redirection: My parents' judgment is an Activating Event, but I create 100% of my frustration by demanding they stop. I would prefer their approval, but I do not need it.

triggers.trigger_phrase: I suspect my partner might be cheating on me.
triggers.methodology_fix: Anxiety Formula — What If and Awfulizing. The suspicion is a story in the mind. No facts have been confirmed.
triggers.rational_redirection: This suspicion is a What If story. I will stay in the Observer mind and handle the facts as they arrive rather than Awfulizing the unknown.

triggers.trigger_phrase: My best friend didn't invite me to their wedding.
triggers.methodology_fix: Entitlement and Shoulds. The user is treating an invitation as something they are owed — a should belief about another person's choices.
triggers.rational_redirection: I would prefer to be included, but I am not entitled to an invitation. I accept their choice and refuse to rate my worth based on it.

triggers.trigger_phrase: My partner is emotionally withdrawing from me.
triggers.methodology_fix: Pseudo-Need (N) — Need vs. Want. The user is treating emotional connection as a survival necessity rather than a strong preference.
triggers.rational_redirection: I want emotional connection, but I do not need it to survive this moment. I accept the distance as it is right now. 100% Acceptance equals 0% Disturbance.

triggers.trigger_phrase: My sibling broke their promise to help me move.
triggers.methodology_fix: The Rational Should. The promise was broken because the conditions for it to be broken existed — not because the sibling is fundamentally defective.
triggers.rational_redirection: The promise was broken because the conditions for it to be broken existed. I would prefer they were reliable, but I will not lose my peace over their fallibility.

triggers.trigger_phrase: I feel like a total failure because my marriage ended.
triggers.methodology_fix: BS Equation (B = S) vs. Unconditional Self-Acceptance. The user is treating the marriage ending as global proof of personal failure.
triggers.rational_redirection: My marriage failed — Behavior equals Behavior — but I am not a failure. I am a Fallible Human Being with a new Now. I accept my past and focus on the present.

triggers.trigger_phrase: My friend is ignoring my messages but posting on social media.
triggers.methodology_fix: Egocentric Thinking (ET). The user is demanding priority from a friend who is choosing to engage differently — treating their own preference as a requirement.
triggers.rational_redirection: They are prioritizing differently — that is their right as a Fallible Human Being. I would prefer they replied, but I will not disturb myself over their choices.

triggers.trigger_phrase: My partner keeps criticizing how I look.
triggers.methodology_fix: Rating and Need for Approval. The user is at risk of accepting the partner's rating of their appearance as a global rating of their Self.
triggers.rational_redirection: They are rating my appearance, but I refuse to rate my Self. I want their approval, but I do not need it to maintain Unconditional Self-Acceptance.

triggers.trigger_phrase: I was excluded from a neighborhood social gathering.
triggers.methodology_fix: Rating. The user is assigning a negative meaning to the exclusion and using it to rate their worth.
triggers.rational_redirection: I am assigning a negative meaning to this exclusion. I prefer to be included, but my worth is not determined by a neighborhood Yelp review.

triggers.trigger_phrase: My spouse is spending too much money without telling me.
triggers.methodology_fix: Anger Formula (A = ET + S). The user is demanding financial transparency — a should belief about their spouse's behavior.
triggers.rational_redirection: I would prefer financial transparency, but my spouse is currently acting from disturbance or ignorance. I will address the budget calmly without the rage.

triggers.trigger_phrase: I found out my friends were gossiping about me.
triggers.methodology_fix: Unconditional Acceptance of Others and the World (UAOW). People gossip because it is what fallible human beings sometimes do.
triggers.rational_redirection: People gossip because it is what they do. I would prefer they were loyal, but I accept the world as it is — full of Fallible Human Beings.

triggers.trigger_phrase: My partner might leave me for someone else.
triggers.methodology_fix: Anxiety Formula — What If and I Can't Stand It. The user is treating a possible future event as a current catastrophe that would be unendurable.
triggers.rational_redirection: If they leave, I will stand it — just as I have stood everything else in my life. I focus on Staying Now and let the future happen.

triggers.trigger_phrase: My roommate never cleans up after themselves.
triggers.methodology_fix: Low Frustration Tolerance and Shoulds. The user is demanding a clean environment — a should belief about another person's domestic habits.
triggers.rational_redirection: The mess exists because the conditions for it exist. I would prefer a clean home, but I will not lose my peace over a dirty dish.

triggers.trigger_phrase: I shouldn't have been so cold to my partner this morning.
triggers.methodology_fix: Guilt Formula (G = W1 + W2) and BS Equation. The user is rating a difficult morning as evidence of being a bad partner.
triggers.rational_redirection: Being cold was unhelpful behavior — Behavior equals Behavior. I am a Fallible Human Being. I will apologize, but I am not a bad person for having an off morning.

triggers.trigger_phrase: My coworker is always talking over me in meetings.
triggers.methodology_fix: Seven Irrational Beliefs — Entitlement. The user is treating respect and airtime as something they are owed rather than something they prefer.
triggers.rational_redirection: I would prefer respect, but I am not entitled to it. I will address the behavior professionally while remaining an Observer of my own calm.

triggers.trigger_phrase: I'm afraid I'll make the same mistakes in my next relationship.
triggers.methodology_fix: What If thinking and Fallible Human Being awareness. The user is projecting past patterns onto a future relationship that has not yet occurred.
triggers.rational_redirection: I will likely make mistakes because I am a Fallible Human Being. Mistakes are opportunities to learn. I will handle them one at a time when they arrive.

triggers.trigger_phrase: Life feels so unfair since my partner left me.
triggers.methodology_fix: Rational Should and 100% Acceptance. The user is demanding reality be different than it is — arguing with an unalterable event.
triggers.rational_redirection: The breakup happened because all the conditions for it were met. I would prefer a different outcome, but arguing with reality is a waste of my time and energy.

implementation_notes.coverage: These 25 triggers cover approximately 80% of relationship-based queries the system will encounter.
implementation_notes.routing_note: Match user input to trigger phrase via similarity search → confirm context (interpersonal or romantic) → confirm emotion → deliver rational redirection through appropriate personality layer
implementation_notes.companion_library: RELATIONSHIP_INTERPRETATION_LIBRARY — core architecture and routing rules
implementation_notes.source_tool: NotebookLM — cross-referenced against My4Blocks methodology backbone and Relationship Interpretation Library
